turns.content: I'm trying to build a simple async executor but I thought if I just store the futures in a Vec and loop through them, they should all run automatically. Is that how it works?

That's a common first guess, but a plain Vec won't work because you need to track when each future is done without blocking the thread. You actually want to use a VecDeque for your task queue so you can pop items off efficiently and add new ones at the back.
Okay, so I put futures in that deque. How do I know when one is ready? Do I just poll every single future until it returns true?

Polling every future on every iteration would be wasteful and slow down your program significantly. Instead, you only poll the specific ones that have registered a waker via Arc to notify when they're ready.
Got it. So I use Arc to share state between threads? And then I check if any of those wakers are triggered?

Exactly, you wrap the future's readiness flag in an Arc so multiple threads can see updates safely. Your loop will keep polling tasks from your VecDeque and only proceed when a Waker signals completion.
I'm still confused about how I handle the single-threaded executor part. Do I just run this main loop forever?
You do keep a main execution loop running in one thread, continuously checking if any Waker has been notified since your last poll cycle.
So I need to store the futures in that VecDeque and wait for wakers before moving on?
Yes, your executor maintains a single-threaded loop where you poll only those futures whose Waker has been signaled, avoiding unnecessary work.
One more thing - how do I know when the entire set of tasks is finished? Is there a flag I check?
You can track the total number of futures you started and count how many have completed via their Waker. When that counter equals your initial task list size, you know the executor is done.
